prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, low-contrast product shot. Several tall bottles or containers appear to be arranged upright, with two central bottles most visible. Their labels are faintly visible but not clearly readable in this frame. The overall image is shadowy, making details of the packaging and background difficult to distinguish.

By the final frame, the visual style has changed to a bright, warm-toned close-up of a smiling blonde woman looking toward the camera. Her face fills most of the frame, with soft golden lighting and a blurred yellow-green background. In the lower left, large on-screen text reads: “A Little Touch Of Country.” In the lower right, there is a small inset image showing two product bottles side by side, with labels similar to those seen in the opening frame. The exact product name and details on the bottles are not fully legible, but the packaging is prominently displayed as part of the final composition.
